content.statement: Passive contraction is genuinely policy-neutral — i.e., no active government lever (such as the score-threshold mechanism documented in S2) is simultaneously operating to accelerate or shape the contraction beyond pure demographic effect — though the demography record itself notes government is currently using score thresholds as an additional supply-side lever, meaning a truly 'passive' baseline may not exist in the current policy environment and this scenario should be read as an analytical counterfactual rather than an achievable real-world state.
